description: ROUND 4: Add EvolutionMetrics, confidence scoring, failure categorization, and revert_if_worse logic to the evolution engine and reflector. Currently the reflector just counts failures without understanding WHY, and there is no A/B performance comparison.
steps: Create kernel/evolution/metrics.py with EvolutionMetrics class that: tracks success_rate, avg_retries, avg_duration per node over a sliding window (configurable, default 10 iterations). Methods: record_iteration(node_id, success, retries, duration), get_node_metrics(node_id) -> dict with success_rate/avg_retries/avg_duration, get_overall_health() -> float (0.0-1.0 composite score).

In kernel/reflector.py, add failure categorization: add categorize_failure(errors, result) method that returns one of: 'timeout', 'test_failure', 'code_error', 'dependency_issue', 'unknown'. Heuristic: if 'timeout' in any error -> timeout, if 'test' in any error -> test_failure, if 'import' or 'dependency' in any error -> dependency_issue, if 'syntax' or 'error' in any error -> code_error, else unknown.

Update Reflector.propose_evolution() to include a confidence_score (0.0-1.0) in each proposal based on: number of data points (more data = higher confidence), consistency of failures (all same category = higher), and recency of failures (more recent = higher).

In kernel/evolution/engine.py, add revert_if_worse(change_id, metrics_before, metrics_after, threshold=0.1) method that: compares node metrics before and after a change was applied. If success_rate dropped by more than threshold, auto-rollback the change and return True (reverted). Otherwise return False.
Update Reflector.propose_evolution() to include 'failure_category' field in each proposal based on categorize_failure() of the most common failure type for that node.

Write tests in tests/test_evolution.py (new class TestEvolutionMetrics) covering: record_iteration updates metrics, get_node_metrics returns correct averages, sliding window works, get_overall_health returns reasonable score. Write tests in tests/test_reflector.py (new class TestFailureCategorization) covering: each failure category is correctly identified, confidence_score is included in proposals, failure_category is included. Write tests for revert_if_worse in tests/test_evolution.py.
acceptance_criteria: EvolutionMetrics correctly tracks per-node performance over sliding window
categorize_failure() correctly classifies different error types
propose_evolution() includes confidence_score and failure_category in proposals
revert_if_worse() auto-rollbacks changes that degrade performance
verification: eval "$(pyenv init -)" && pyenv shell 3.12.13 && python -m pytest tests/ --cov=. --cov-report=term-missing --tb=short -q
findings: All 261 tests pass (up from 244 baseline). Coverage at 99%. Added 17 new tests across TestEvolutionMetrics, TestRevertIfWorse, and TestFailureCategorization classes. No existing tests broken.
